steps: Find a common denominator for \(\frac{4}{4x-5} - \frac{1}{x+1}\). The LCD is \((4x-5)(x+1)\).

• Choice A — '\(\frac{1}{(x+1)(4x-5)}\)': The numerator after combining is 9, not 1. ✗
  • Choice B — '\(\frac{3}{3x-6}\)': The denominator does not factor to \(3x-6\); this is not equivalent. ✗
  • Choice C — '\(-\frac{1}{(x+1)(4x-5)}\)': Numerator is positive 9, not −1. ✗
  • Choice D — '\(\frac{9}{(x+1)(4x-5)}\)': Matches the combined expression exactly. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
